description_long: This is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a tall, slender pole with a square base for stability. The signal head features three circular lenses arranged vertically, with the top lens illuminated in red, while the other two are unlit. The housing of the signal is rectangular with a slightly arched top, and it appears to be constructed from a weathered, dark-colored metal, possibly steel or aluminum, with some visible surface mottling. Below the signal lights, there is a rectangular sign with alphanumeric markings, likely for identification or location reference. The pole is metallic and cylindrical, supporting the signal head at a height suitable for visibility to drivers. The overall design is utilitarian, robust, and typical for deployment at intersections or road crossings in urban environments.
description: A tall, pole-mounted traffic light with three vertically stacked signal lenses and a weathered metal housing, featuring a red light illuminated at the top and an identification sign below.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three signal lenses, with the top red light illuminated and a clear identification sign below.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the signal housing and the protrusion of the lenses, with the pole and base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the signal housing, which is flat and featureless, with the pole and base in full view.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the side profile of the signal housing and the pole.
